name: The Boiler House
description: A low brick outbuilding attached to the tower's south face, containing the coal-fired boiler that feeds steam to the pump room below. The Boiler_Steam_Pressure gauge is mounted on the firebox door frame, currently reading 45 PSI.
atmosphericDetails: The smell of wet coal dust and heated lubricating grease is overwhelming within two steps of the door — it coats the back of the throat.
The gauge glass shows the water level at three-quarters — adequate, but the level is dropping faster than the coal input rate should allow.
A pipe wrench has been left on the floor beside the main steam admission valve, its jaws set to a diameter that matches the valve's packing nut.
